title: Stenostephanus magdalenensis
intro: Stenostephanus magdalenensis, also known as Magdalena's Blue Porterweed, is a plant species belonging to the Acanthaceae family, characterized by its small, blue-purple flowers and narrow leaves. Traditionally, the plant has been used to treat various health issues, including fever, cough, and respiratory problems, due to its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of Stenostephanus magdalenensis include flavonoids, glycosides, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, the plant is often prepared as an infusion or a tincture, and is commonly used to treat respiratory infections, such as bronchitis and pneumonia.
